name: Idaho Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Idaho - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Bureau of Occupational Licenses — Speech and Hearing Services

address.description:
Bureau of Occupational Licenses
700 W. State St.
Boise, Idaho 83702

requiredDocuments.name: Praxis exam score report
requiredDocuments.description: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does an Idaho SLP license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The application and license fee is $75. Idaho has among the lowest SLP licensing fees. Annual renewal is $75.

mainEntity.name: Is Idaho part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Idaho has enacted ASLP-IC legislation.

mainEntity.name: Does Idaho allow telepractice for SLPs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Idaho permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and compact privilege holders.

mainEntity.name: Does Idaho have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Idaho participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Idaho without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Idaho?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Idaho while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
